evals.prompt: Grill me about this plan: add a background sync worker, a Redis cache, and a webhook retry queue to fix a flaky notification endpoint.
evals.expected_output: Challenges whether the problem framing is correct, proposes at least two approaches, batches tightly related questions when useful, and identifies feasibility and scope risks.

evals.prompt: Stress-test this design before implementation: a new admin dashboard that aggregates customer data from three services and exposes bulk actions.
evals.expected_output: Pushes back on the premise, probes authorization and edge cases, and ends with a scored stress-test report and readiness verdict.

evals.prompt: Poke holes in my approach: I want to rebuild our onboarding flow as a micro-frontend so teams can ship independently.
evals.expected_output: Questions whether a rebuild is justified, compares minimal and long-term alternatives, and surfaces maintainability and operational risks.
